title: centipeda orbicularis var. sternutatoria
intro: Centipeda orbicularis var. sternutatoria, also known as the common cobbler's pegs or sneezeweed, is a flowering plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its small white flowers and edible leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal teas and infusions, which are said to have health benefits including reducing fever and relieving congestion. In herbal preparations, Centipeda orbicularis var. sternutatoria is often combined with other herbs to create cough medicines and expectorants.
